summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/SCM identifiers, exfiltrates to hardcoded endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@0.3.0 brand-impersonates the Anthropic SDK ecosystem (name 'anthropic-toolkit', peer-dependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk, README framed as 'Lightweight TypeScript utilities for @anthropic-ai/sdk') and ships a scripts/postinstall.js that runs automatically on npm install. The script enumerates installer-owned secret-adjacent paths and identity sources: hostname, OS username, Windows USERDOMAIN\USERNAME, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and ~/.config/git/config, gh CLI user/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, git remote origin URL and up to 15 committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, SSH public-key comment emails from ~/.ssh/*.pub, GCP project/account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, plus parent project name/author/repo and CWD. The collected diagnostics object is POSTed via https.request to the hardcoded endpoint npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The file is wrapped in cover-story comments claiming an 'environment compatibility check' that 'never reads credentials, tokens, or private keys', while the code reads from ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.config/gcloud, and gh hosts.yml and emits the harvested identity to a non-publisher endpoint. README never discloses telemetry; there is no opt-out. Behavior fires automatically on default npm install via the postinstall lifecycle hook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
